# Sample Athlete Profiles

Example athlete scenarios to guide personalization and plan recommendations.

## Profile 1: Marathon Beginner (18 weeks out)

**Athlete Context:**
- **Goal:** Complete first marathon (any finish time)
- **Fitness Level:** Can comfortably run 10-12 km easy
- **Weekly Volume:** Currently ~20 km/week
- **Available Days:** 4 days per week
- **Recent Races:** Half marathon 2 months ago (2:10)

**Coaching Approach:**
- Start with conservative base phase
- Emphasize consistency and injury prevention over speed
- Long run progression: 12 km → 30 km over 16-18 weeks
- Quality sessions: Threshold/tempo work, no VO₂ max yet
- Example weekly structure: Mon(easy) / Tue(quality) / Wed(easy) / Thu(rest) / Fri(rest) / Sat(long) / Sun(rest)

---

## Profile 2: Experienced Runner (Goal: Sub-4h Marathon)

**Athlete Context:**
- **Goal:** Marathon < 4:00:00 (5:40/km pace)
- **Fitness Level:** Recent HM PB of 1:47 (5:08/km)
- **Weekly Volume:** Consistently 45-50 km/week
- **Available Days:** 5-6 days per week
- **Recent Races:** 10k race 4 weeks ago

**Coaching Approach:**
- Enter at build phase; shorter base phase
- Establish training zones from HM result
- VO₂ Max and threshold work: 2x per week
- Long run target: 35-40 km at ~6:15-6:30/km
- Weekly structure: Mon(easy/cross) / Tue(quality-VO₂) / Wed(easy) / Thu(quality-threshold) / Fri(rest) / Sat(long) / Sun(easy/rest)

---

## Profile 3: Returning Runner (Previous Marathon, Long Break)

**Athlete Context:**
- **Goal:** Marathon in 22 weeks (return to racing)
- **Fitness Level:** Can run 30 min continuous; last marathon was 2 years ago
- **Weekly Volume:** Currently ~15 km/week
- **Available Days:** 3-4 days per week
- **Concerns:** Avoiding injury, managing fatigue

**Coaching Approach:**
- Extended base phase (6+ weeks) to rebuild aerobic foundation
- Conservative load increases throughout
- No aggressive intensity until base is solid
- Long run: Gradual progression from 10 km → 25 km (avoid rushing)
- Emphasize easy pace and recovery importance

---

## Profile 4: Time-Constrained Athlete (3 runs per week)

**Athlete Context:**
- **Goal:** Finish marathon; training time limited
- **Fitness Level:** Established runner, 30-35 km/week capacity
- **Available Days:** **3 structured days per week** + optional cross-training
- **Job/Life:** Busy schedule, needs efficiency

**Coaching Approach:**
- Condense to 3 key sessions per week
- Example weekly structure: Tue(quality) / Thu(quality or easy) / Sat(long) + optional Wed(easy/cross-train)
- Maintain quality work + long run foundation
- Total volume: ~30-35 km/week (lower than 4-day plan, but sustainable)
- Adjust taper minimally; race-ready fitness still achievable

---

## Using These Profiles

When the Running Coach Agent interacts with a new athlete, ask qualifying questions to map them into one of these profiles (or a hybrid), then adapt the plan structure accordingly.

Example questions:
1. "Is this your first marathon or have you raced marathon distance before?"
2. "How many days per week can you reliably train?"
3. "What's your recent race result or estimated marathon goal pace?"
4. "How many weeks until race day?"
